page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor, utilizing the arm64 architecture. It features 192 dedicated physical cores running at 2.7 GHz with a single thread per core, backed by 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The server operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 314,716.71 and a top-tier passmark CPU physics score. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 959.17 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 434.92 GB/sec for large block write operations. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation with Clang also rank in the top tier. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category.

The server exhibits a clear tradeoff between massive multi-core scaling and single-threaded execution speeds. While it achieves top-tier throughput in static web serving and Redis SET operations, its LLM inference speed for small model prompt processing is weak. The hardware profile is optimized for highly parallelized workloads such as software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving, rather than single-threaded tasks or GPU-dependent workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.48xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 384.0 GB of memory, this Nitro-based instance provides a 2.0 GB per core ratio and 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core capabilities, including exceptional memory bandwidth, software compilation speeds, and database operations. Conversely, single-core performance and small-model LLM prompt processing remain average to weak. This server is designed for highly parallelized workloads, such as multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, and database management, where its high core density and memory throughput can be fully leveraged.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.48xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server with 192 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 384 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.48xlarge is an arm64 server featuring 192 dedicated Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated cores and high-bandwidth memory for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores on arm64 architecture
384.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations
Average single-core performance and weak small-model LLM prompt processing
Suited for highly parallelized workloads, static web serving, and Redis deployments
